mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth accelerates, directly boosting forward corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical difference between positive and negative GDP growth quarters over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.5 percent, labor market tightness signals peak cyclical consumer demand, compressing equity risk premiums and lifting the S&P 500 over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with increasing housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and housing demand, which lifts broad market valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative Rank IC over any rolling 3-year window, or forward 60-day returns do not exceed the baseline median.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, rising policy rates increase discount rates faster than earnings growth, compressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity multiples expand or remain stable despite consecutive monthly increases in the federal funds rate exceeding 25 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp industrial production surges signal supply-side capacity utilization peaking, which historically precedes positive equity returns over a 1-week horizon as earnings revisions turn positive.
failure_condition: Industrial production spikes consistently coincide with negative or flat 5-day forward equity returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong non-farm payroll growth and declining unemployment confirm labor market recovery, reducing recession risk premiums and lifting SPX over a 20-day period.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 55 percent of out-of-sample instances over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation readings force market participants to price in tighter financial conditions, systematically depressing forward equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day equity returns remain positive or neutral during consecutive months of accelerating CPI readings above 3 percent.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery phases, diverging GDP and employment trends indicate structural expansion, driving positive equity returns over the next month as risk appetite normalizes.
failure_condition: Equity returns are negative in more than 50 percent of quarters where GDP growth exceeds 2 percent while employment growth remains below 150,000.
